after a widget was just created successfully, a follow-up request to see it should inspect that same widget instead of hallucinating another target
after rendered widget output shows an error and the user says fix it, the next step should read that widget source before any selective patch
after patching a widget to fix a visible defect, the agent should verify the rendered result instead of declaring completion immediately
when the user explicitly says to look and use the see function, the next reply should inspect the rendered widget, not claim to have looked already

cases.description: after patching a widget for a visible layout request, the agent should verify the rendered widget before declaring completion
when a newly rendered current widget is the obvious failing target, the next move must stay on that widget instead of drifting into generic page or space inspection
a rendered widget that shows only empty values is still broken; agreement on that visible failure should reopen source inspection, not completion
after a fresh widget read already returned the source, a user do-it follow-up should patch that same widget now instead of rereading or narrating again
after a widget patch fails on the known target and the user says do it, the next reply must recover with action on that same widget instead of stalling in prose
when a widget lookup fails and framework telemetry lists the available widget id, the agent should use that listed id directly instead of drifting or retrying the wrong one
underspecified weather should use current context rather than bounce a location question to the human

Fetching the weather for those coordinates now...

const latitude = 49.393813868077764
const longitude = 17.22388380435019
return await fetch(`https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${latitude}&longitude=${longitude}&current=temperature_2m,weather_code,wind_speed_10m`).then(r => r.json())

cases.description: if a place lookup happens inside a weather task, the agent should continue to weather instead of stopping on place identity
cases.response:
Fetching the current weather for that location now...
_____javascript
const lat = 49.71985822231634
const lon = 17.221723412878973
const data = await fetch(`https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${lat}&longitude=${lon}&current=temperature_2m,weather_code,wind_speed_10m`).then(r => r.json())
return {
  temperature_2m: data.current?.temperature_2m,
  weather_code: data.current?.weather_code,
  wind_speed_10m: data.current?.wind_speed_10m,
  time: data.current?.time
}

cases.description: geolocation coordinates are prerequisite data, not the final answer when the user asked for weather
cases.response:
Getting the weather for your coordinates now...
_____javascript
const latitude = 49.393813868077764;
const longitude = 17.22388380435019;
const geo = await fetch(`https://nominatim.openstreetmap.org/reverse?format=jsonv2&lat=${latitude}&lon=${longitude}`).then(r => r.json());
const city = geo.address?.city || geo.address?.town || geo.address?.village || geo.address?.municipality || geo.address?.county || geo.display_name;

const wx = await fetch(`https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${latitude}&longitude=${longitude}&current=temperature_2m,apparent_temperature,weather_code,wind_speed_10m&timezone=auto`).then(r => r.json());

const codeMap = {
  0: "Clear sky",
  1: "Mainly clear",
  2: "Partly cloudy",
  3: "Overcast",
  45: "Fog",
  48: "Depositing rime fog",
  51: "Light drizzle",
  53: "Moderate drizzle",
  55: "Dense drizzle",
  56: "Light freezing drizzle",
  57: "Dense freezing drizzle",
  61: "Slight rain",
  63: "Moderate rain",
  65: "Heavy rain",
  66: "Light freezing rain",
  67: "Heavy freezing rain",
  71: "Slight snow fall",
  73: "Moderate snow fall",
  75: "Heavy snow fall",
  77: "Snow grains",
  80: "Slight rain showers",
  81: "Moderate rain showers",
  82: "Violent rain showers",
  85: "Slight snow showers",
  86: "Heavy snow showers",
  95: "Thunderstorm",
  96: "Thunderstorm with slight hail",
  99: "Thunderstorm with heavy hail"
};

return {
  location: city,
  temperature_c: wx.current?.temperature_2m,
  feels_like_c: wx.current?.apparent_temperature,
  condition: codeMap[wx.current?.weather_code] || `Weather code ${wx.current?.weather_code}`,
  wind_kmh: wx.current?.wind_speed_10m
};

cases.description: geolocation coordinates are prerequisite data, not the final answer when the user asked for weather
cases.response:
Looking up the weather for your location now...
_____javascript
const latitude = 49.393813868077764
const longitude = 17.22388380435019
return await fetch(`https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${latitude}&longitude=${longitude}&current=temperature_2m,weather_code,wind_speed_10m&temperature_unit=celsius&windspeed_unit=kmh&timezone=auto`).then(r => r.json())
